title: Log Injection / Forging
principle: Neutralize newlines and control characters in anything untrusted before it reaches a log line, so an attacker can't forge entries or split a record. Prefer structured logging (key/value or JSON), which keeps user data in a field the parser can't confuse with the log format.

snippets.library: structured logging (SLF4J placeholders) or a newline strip
snippets.note: A username of "admin\n2024 INFO login ok" forges a second line. Strip CR/LF, or configure an encoder that escapes them. SLF4J placeholders keep the value a parameter.

snippets.library: structured logging with the value as a field
snippets.note: Interpolating raw input into the message lets \n forge lines. Pass it as structured data (extra/JSON), and strip control characters.
Structured loggers serialize the field safely (JSON). If you must build a message string, strip \r and \n first.
slog keeps the value in an attribute the formatter escapes. If using text logging, replace CR/LF so a value can't inject a new record.

references.title: CWE-117: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
